question.text: When did NSE launch its currency futures trading platform in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NSE launched currency futures in August 2008 in a separate currency derivatives segment. Initially only USD-INR futures were available; EUR-INR, GBP-INR, and JPY-INR were added from February 2010. **The Trap:** February 2010 is when non-USD INR pairs were added, not the initial launch. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers equity derivatives launch history. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Currency futures preceded cross-currency pairs (EUR-USD, GBP-USD, USD-JPY) which were introduced only in 2018.
